key_departures.new_team: TBD (Ravens trade fell through — failed physical; Crosby remains on Raiders roster)
key_departures.impact: Raiders agreed to trade Crosby to Baltimore for two first-round picks (2026 No. 14, 2027 1st), but Ravens backed out after Crosby failed his physical on March 11. Crosby underwent meniscus repair surgery in January. He remains under contract with Las Vegas, but his status and relationship with the team are deeply complicated. If he is eventually traded or remains on the roster under uncertain health, this is the defining EDGE situation of the offseason. His 2025 season ended on IR with a knee injury and 10 sacks.

One of three starting LBs entering free agency. White was a one-year prove-it signing in 2025 who started the majority of games. Departure leaves a hole that the Raiders addressed aggressively in free agency.
Second starting LB departing. Along with White and Adams, the entire 2025 LB trio is gone. Created a critical need at the position.
Adams transitioned to linebacker and was third in the starting rotation. Not high-priority for Las Vegas but his departure further emptied the LB room.

Raiders traded Smith and a 2026 7th-round pick to the Jets for a 2026 6th-round pick. Smith was the projected starter before the Raiders pivoted to tanking for Fernando Mendoza. The Raiders hold the No. 1 overall pick and are expected to draft Mendoza, making Smith's departure a planned move, not a loss.
Starting guard released to clear cap space. Left a hole at guard alongside the existing center need.
8-year Raiders kicker effectively replaced by signing of Matt Gay. Carlson's FG% had declined to 81.5% in 2025 (29th in NFL). Team chose to move on. Not a need position for mock draft purposes.
Backup QB signing to Carolina for depth. Raiders acquired Pickett in a trade last offseason but are moving on. With Mendoza expected at No. 1, a bridge/veteran QB will still be needed.

signings.analysis: Record-setting deal — Linderbaum became the highest-paid interior offensive lineman in NFL history at signing. $60M guaranteed on a 3-year deal is a massive commitment. The 25-year-old (turns 26) started 66 games in 4 seasons with Baltimore and is elite in run blocking and the pass-rush operation critical to new HC Klint Kubiak's offense. The Cappa release had created a center/guard void; this fully addresses center. Jackson Powers-Johnson now solidified at RG. IOL drops from a top-2 need to depth/guard only.

Acquired via trade (Raiders sent 2026 6th, received Johnson + 2026 7th). Johnson is signed through 2027 with a $8.67M cap hit in 2026 and $10M in 2027. He is a former 2nd-Team All-Pro nickel corner (2023) who immediately slots into the slot CB role. However, this only addresses the nickel — the Raiders still need outside CB depth. Contract structure (pre-existing deal absorbed, not a new guaranteed commitment) signals a starter-level value at a reasonable price but not a long-term CB1 investment. CB need partially addressed at nickel, outside CB need remains.

Re-signing Stokes keeps the former Packers 1st-round pick who started a career-high 16 games for Las Vegas in 2025 with an OPR of 77.7. $20M guaranteed on a 3-year deal is a real commitment. He is the CB1 outside. Combined with Johnson at nickel, Las Vegas now has a functional CB duo. However, CB depth behind them is thin and no elite CB1 was acquired. CB need moves from tier 1 critical to tier 3 depth-only need.

Paye is a 2021 first-round pick who totaled 30.5 sacks across 5 seasons in Indianapolis but had a down 2025 (4 sacks). $32M guaranteed signals starter-level investment. He addresses the EDGE vacancy created by the Crosby trade drama, but the situation remains complicated: Crosby failed his physical and is still on the roster, creating an uncertain EDGE room. Paye alone does not fully solve the pass-rush need — ESPN's beat reporter noted Las Vegas will need another EDGE opposite Paye. If Crosby is eventually moved, EDGE remains a real need in the draft. Partially addressed.

One-year deal signals a prove-it contract for Koonce, who missed all of 2024 with a knee injury but returned for 4.5 sacks in 2025. He has a relationship with DC Rob Leonard and fits the 3-4 scheme. However, a 1-year deal with reported $0 guaranteed at signing is a bridge — this is not a long-term EDGE solution. With Paye also signed, Koonce gives depth and upside on a short commitment. EDGE need minimally reduced by this signing alone.

Three-year deal with $20M guaranteed is a real starter commitment for the Super Bowl champion LB. Dean brings coverage ability and pass-rush upside (7.5 career sacks) that had been absent from the Raiders' LB room. Injury history (patellar tendon in 2024 playoffs, foot in 2023) is a risk, but at 25 he is young. Combined with Walker below, this is a complete overhaul of the LB room. LB need fully addressed.

Largest contract of the two LB signings — $28M guaranteed makes this a clear long-term starter investment. Walker recorded 100+ tackles in each of his 4 Packers seasons, with a career-high 128 in 2025. He is a thumper against the run and fits the 3-4 OLB role under DC Rob Leonard. Coverage limitations are real but offset by Dean's coverage skills next to him. Walker + Dean = complete LB rebuild from a position that was a top-2 need entering the offseason. Raiders now have two of the ten highest-paid off-ball LBs in the NFL.

Three-year deal with $23M guaranteed positions Nailor as the intended WR1 for incoming rookie QB Fernando Mendoza. However, his production is limited — he has never exceeded 29 catches or 444 yards in a single season, largely due to being buried behind Jefferson and Addison in Minnesota. He is a slot/vertical threat with 14.8-15.3 YPC over the past two seasons. The Raiders' WR room (Tucker, Bech, Thornton, Nailor) is still thin — analysts note more additions are needed and WR remains a viable draft target. Partially addressed — the room has a clear starter now, but the group still lacks a true WR1 in terms of production resume.

One-year deal — contract financials not yet publicly disclosed. Gay replaced Daniel Carlson, who is now an unsigned free agent. Gay made his 2021 Pro Bowl with the Rams under new special teams coordinator Joe DeCamillis, reuniting the tandem. However, Gay was 73.9% on FGs in 2025 (only 2 games for SF after being released by Washington). This is a lateral-to-downgrade replacement at K from a pure stats standpoint. Not a draft position of need.
Exclusive Rights Free Agent tendered at league minimum. Snowden is a developmental DE/EDGE piece. No meaningful impact on positional need.
ERFA tender at league minimum. Depth safety retained. No need impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Tyler Linderbaum signing (3yr/$81M, $60M gtd) makes center a solved problem for 3 years. Jackson Powers-Johnson returns healthy at RG. The Cappa release created a guard void but Powers-Johnson fills it. At most, a developmental guard on Day 3 of the draft. IOL is no longer a premium need.

The Raiders completely rebuilt their LB room: Walker (3yr/$40.5M, $28M gtd) + Dean (3yr/$36M, $20M gtd) represent two multi-year starter commitments totaling $76.5M. All three 2025 starters (White, Roberts, Adams) were allowed to walk but were replaced by significantly better players. LB is no longer a need for the 2026 draft.

Stokes re-sign (3yr/$30M, $20M gtd) secures the CB1 outside. Taron Johnson trade adds a former 2nd-Team All-Pro nickel corner. The Raiders now have a functional CB starting duo. However, CB depth behind Stokes/Johnson is thin, and no elite lockdown CB1 was acquired. CB remains a tier 3 depth need — a Day 2 cornerback in the draft would still be welcome.

The EDGE situation is uniquely complicated. Paye (3yr/$48M, $32M gtd) is a real starter commitment, and Koonce returns on a 1-year deal. However, Maxx Crosby failed his Ravens physical on March 11 and remains on the Raiders roster in an unclear status — his trade market will likely reset at a lower price. The team may still seek to move Crosby (health risk, relationship strain). Even with Paye + Koonce, the Raiders lack an elite proven pass-rusher opposite. ESPN's beat reporter noted Las Vegas will need another EDGE opposite Paye. EDGE remains a tier 2 need — a premium EDGE in the draft (now at No. 14, not No. 1) is realistic and likely.

Nailor (3yr/$35M, $23M gtd) gives the Raiders a WR1-caliber salary player but unproven WR1-caliber production. He has never caught more than 29 balls in a season. The room of Nailor + Tucker + Bech + Thornton is improved but still thin for a rebuilding team expecting a rookie QB. WR stays at tier 2 — a WR in the draft (particularly at No. 14 or in rounds 2-3) is still a strong possibility and need.

The Raiders hold the No. 1 overall pick and are widely expected to select Indiana QB Fernando Mendoza. The QB need is effectively solved via the draft, not free agency. Geno Smith was traded to the Jets and Pickett departed to Carolina, leaving no veteran QB on roster yet. A veteran bridge/backup QB is still needed (team reportedly exploring options), but QB as a draft need is resolved. Tier 3 reflects the backup/bridge QB gap that still exists.

No meaningful IDL additions in free agency. The interior defensive line remains a need heading into the draft. Thomas Booker IV was tendered as an RFA, providing modest depth continuity, but no starter-level IDL was signed. Need unchanged.
No offensive tackle signings in free agency. Kolton Miller returns healthy at LT but missed significant time in 2025. Opposite tackle is still a concern. OT need unchanged.
No safety signed beyond ERFA tender of Tristin McCollum at the minimum. Lonnie Johnson Jr. is a UFA. The Raiders' safety room has not been addressed. Need unchanged.

key_additions.headline: Record-setting center signing ($60M gtd) instantly upgrades the offensive line and Kubiak's presnap operation for rookie QB Fernando Mendoza
100+ tackle machine in each of his 4 NFL seasons anchors a completely rebuilt LB room alongside college teammate Nakobe Dean
Super Bowl champion LB brings pass-rush ability and coverage skills to erase the Raiders' most glaring defensive need
30.5 career sacks in 5 seasons with Colts steps into the pass-rush void created by Maxx Crosby's failed trade
Former 2nd-Team All-Pro nickel corner acquired for a 6th-round pick, immediately upgrades the slot coverage unit
Raiders retain their CB1 with a 3-year commitment, locking in the former 1st-round pick who started all 16 games in 2025
Speedy homecoming signing (Las Vegas native) becomes the Raiders' de facto WR1 ahead of Mendoza's expected arrival
Raiders retain the 4.5-sack pass rusher on a prove-it deal as he continues his comeback from a 2024 torn ACL

key_losses.contract_summary: Trade to Ravens collapsed — failed physical; remains on Raiders roster
key_losses.headline: Blockbuster Ravens trade dissolved after Crosby failed his physical (January meniscus repair); franchise icon's future in Las Vegas deeply uncertain
Veteran QB traded away as Raiders prepare to draft Fernando Mendoza No. 1 overall and enter a full franchise rebuild
Starting guard released in cap cleanup, accelerating the offensive line overhaul that led to the Linderbaum signing
One of three starting LBs who walked in free agency, triggering the Raiders' twin Dean and Walker signings

key_losses.contract_summary: UFA — replaced by Matt Gay signing
key_losses.headline: Eight-year Raiders franchise kicker effectively replaced as new regime moves on from one of the last Oakland holdovers
Former safety-turned-linebacker returns to the team that drafted him, completing the Raiders' complete LB room turnover

notes: The Maxx Crosby situation is the most significant unresolved item: the Ravens backed out of the agreed trade (2026 No. 14 pick + 2027 1st) on March 11 after Crosby failed his physical. Crosby (meniscus repair, January 2026) remains on the Raiders roster. His trade market will reset at a lower ask. His status — whether traded, retained, or released — is the most critical remaining EDGE decision and may shift EDGE tier significantly.
Geno Smith trade (to Jets for a 6th) is confirmed. Raiders receive a 2026 6th in return. A veteran bridge QB is still needed as Fernando Mendoza's backup.
Matt Gay contract financials were not publicly disclosed as of March 11, 2026. The one-year deal is confirmed; raw dollar values listed as 0 pending disclosure.
Malcolm Koonce re-signing reported with $0 guaranteed by available sources — listed accordingly. Actual guarantee structure may be slightly different pending official filing.
Thomas Booker IV and Jordan Meredith were tendered as RFAs — not included as meaningful FA signings.

Cap space post-FA per OverTheCap as of March 11, 2026 ($67,610,609). Pre-FA figure based on reported $111.9M (per Over The Cap before signings). Raiders spent approximately $281.5M in total contract value in the first wave per NFL Network.
